info.title: Manus OpenAPI v2
info.description: API for integrating Manus into your workflow. All responses are wrapped with {"ok": true, "request_id": "...", ...} for success and {"ok": false, "request_id": "...", "error": {"code": "...", "message": "..."}} for errors.

post.description: Creates a new task. The task runs asynchronously. Poll for progress with [task.listMessages](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/task.listMessages), send follow-ups with [task.sendMessage](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/task.sendMessage). See the [Task Lifecycle](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/task-lifecycle) guide for the complete flow.

message.description: The message to start the task with. Contains the prompt text, optional file attachments, and connector/skill configuration.

project_id.description: Project ID to associate this task with. The project's instruction will be automatically applied. Use [project.list](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/project.list) to get available project IDs.

locale.description: Locale for the task output language (e.g., "en", "zh-CN", "ja"). Defaults to the user's account locale setting.

interactive_mode.description: When enabled, the agent may pause and ask follow-up questions if the input is insufficient. When disabled (default), the agent proceeds with best-effort execution without asking.

hide_in_task_list.description: When true, the task will not appear in the Manus webapp task list. The task is still accessible via the task_url in the response. Useful for automated/background tasks.

share_visibility.description: Controls who can view the task. "private" (default) — only the task creator can view. "team" — all team members can view. "public" — anyone with the share_url can view without authentication.

agent_profile.description: Agent profile to use for the task. "manus-1.6" (default) — standard capability. "manus-1.6-lite" — lightweight, faster responses. "manus-1.6-max" — maximum capability.

title.description: Custom title for the task. If not provided, a title will be auto-generated based on the input message.

request_id.description: Unique identifier for this API request, useful for debugging.

task_id.description: Unique identifier for the created task. Use this to track, update, or delete the task.

task_title.description: Title for the task. Returns the custom title if one was provided in the request, otherwise an auto-generated title based on the input message.

task_url.description: URL to view the task in the Manus webapp (e.g., https://manus.im/app/{task_id}).

share_url.description: Publicly accessible URL for sharing. Only present when share_visibility is not "private".

share_visibility.description: The actual visibility state of the task.

get.description: Retrieves a task's status and metadata. Use [task.listMessages](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/task.listMessages) for the full event history, or [task.sendMessage](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/task.sendMessage) to continue the conversation.

x-codeSamples.label: cURL
x-codeSamples.source:
curl --request GET \
  --url 'https://api.manus.ai/v2/task.detail?task_id=<string>' \
  --header 'x-manus-api-key: <api-key>'

x-codeSamples.label: Python
x-codeSamples.source:
import requests

url = "https://api.manus.ai/v2/task.detail"

headers = {
    "x-manus-api-key": "<api-key>"
}

response = requests.get(url, headers=headers, params={"task_id": "<string>"})

print(response.text)

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript
PHP
x-codeSamples.source:
<?php

$curl = curl_init();

curl_setopt_array($curl, [
  CURLOPT_URL => "https://api.manus.ai/v2/task.detail?task_id=<string>",
  CURLOPT_RETURNTRANSFER => true,
  CURLOPT_ENCODING => "",
  CURLOPT_MAXREDIRS => 10,
  CURLOPT_TIMEOUT => 30,
  CURLOPT_HTTP_VERSION => CURL_HTTP_VERSION_1_1,
  CURLOPT_CUSTOMREQUEST => "GET",
  CURLOPT_HTTPHEADER => [
    "x-manus-api-key: <api-key>"
  ],
]);

$response = curl_exec($curl);
$err = curl_error($curl);

curl_close($curl);

if ($err) {
  echo "cURL Error #:" . $err;
} else {
  echo $response;
}

x-codeSamples.label: Go
x-codeSamples.source:
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"net/http"
	"io"
)

func main() {

	url := "https://api.manus.ai/v2/task.detail?task_id=<string>"

	req, _ := http.NewRequest("GET", url, nil)

	req.Header.Add("x-manus-api-key", "<api-key>")

	res, _ := http.DefaultClient.Do(req)

	defer res.Body.Close()
	body, _ := io.ReadAll(res.Body)

	fmt.Println(string(body))

}

x-codeSamples.label: Java
Ruby
x-codeSamples.source:
require 'uri'
require 'net/http'

url = URI("https://api.manus.ai/v2/task.detail?task_id=<string>")

http = Net::HTTP.new(url.host, url.port)
http.use_ssl = true

request = Net::HTTP::Get.new(url)
request["x-manus-api-key"] = '<api-key>'

response = http.request(request)
puts response.read_body

parameters.name: task_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the task to retrieve. Supports the shortcut `agent-default-main_task` for the IM agent's main task.

request_id.description: Unique identifier for this API request.

task.description: The task object with current status and metadata.

get.description: Lists tasks with optional filtering and cursor-based pagination. Use `scope` to filter by task type, e.g. `agent_subtask` with `agent_id` for agent subtasks. See [task.detail](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/task.detail) for full details on a specific task.

schema.description: Number of tasks to return per page. Default: 20, Max: 100.
Pagination cursor from the previous response's next_cursor field. Omit for the first page.
Sort direction by creation time. "desc" (default) returns newest first, "asc" returns oldest first.

Filter by task type. "all" (default) returns all tasks. "standard" returns regular tasks. "project" returns tasks within a project (requires project_id). "agent_subtask" returns subtasks created by an agent (requires agent_id).

Filter tasks by agent. Required when scope="agent_subtask". Supports the shortcut `agent-default` for the IM agent. Use [agent.list](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/agent.list) to get available agent IDs.
Filter tasks by project. Required when scope="project". Use [project.list](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/project.list) to get available project IDs.

request_id.description: Unique identifier for this API request.

data.description: Array of task objects matching the filter criteria.

has_more.description: Whether there are more tasks beyond this page. If true, use next_cursor to fetch the next page.

next_cursor.description: Cursor to pass as the cursor parameter for the next page. Only present when has_more is true.

post.description: Stops a running task. The task status will change to `stopped`. A stopped task can still be resumed with [task.sendMessage](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/task.sendMessage).

task_id.description: The unique identifier of the running task to stop. Supports the shortcut `agent-default-main_task` for the IM agent's main task.

request_id.description: Unique identifier for this API request.

post.description: Deletes a task permanently. If the task is still running, use [task.stop](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/task.stop) first.

task_id.description: The unique identifier of the task to delete. Agent-related tasks cannot be deleted.

request_id.description: Unique identifier for this API request.

id.description: The ID of the deleted task.

deleted.description: Always true when the deletion was successful.

post.description: Updates a task's metadata (title, sharing, visibility). Use [task.detail](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/task.detail) to see the current values before updating.

task_id.description: The unique identifier of the task to update. Supports the shortcut `agent-default-main_task` for the IM agent's main task.

title.description: New title for the task. Replaces the auto-generated title.

share_visibility.description: Controls who can view the task. "private" — only the task creator can view. "team" — all team members can view. "public" — anyone with the share_url can view.

enable_visible_in_task_list.description: When true, the task appears in the Manus webapp task list. When false, hides the task from the list (still accessible via direct URL).

request_id.description: Unique identifier for this API request.

task_id.description: The ID of the updated task.

task_title.description: The current title of the task.

task_url.description: URL to view the task in the Manus webapp.

share_url.description: Public share URL. Only present when share_visibility is not "private".

share_visibility.description: The actual visibility state of the task.

post.description: Sends a follow-up message to a task for multi-turn conversation. Use this after [task.create](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/task.create) to continue talking, or to reply when `waiting_for_event_type` is `messageAskUser`. For other waiting types, use [task.confirmAction](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/task.confirmAction) instead.

task_id.description: The unique identifier of the task to send the message to. Supports the shortcut `agent-default-main_task` for the IM agent's main task.

message.description: The follow-up message. Supports the same content formats as task.create (plain text or multi-part with files).

200.description: Message sent successfully. The task will resume processing.

request_id.description: Unique identifier for this API request.

task_id.description: The ID of the task the message was sent to.

get.description: Lists event messages for a task with cursor-based pagination. Use this to poll for progress after [task.create](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/task.create) or [task.sendMessage](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/task.sendMessage). See the [Task Lifecycle](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/task-lifecycle) guide for how to handle each status.

x-codeSamples.label: cURL
x-codeSamples.source:
curl --request GET \
  --url 'https://api.manus.ai/v2/task.listMessages?task_id=<string>&order=desc&limit=10' \
  --header 'x-manus-api-key: <api-key>'

x-codeSamples.label: Python
JavaScript
PHP
x-codeSamples.source:
<?php

$curl = curl_init();

curl_setopt_array($curl, [
  CURLOPT_URL => "https://api.manus.ai/v2/task.listMessages?task_id=<string>&order=desc&limit=10",
  CURLOPT_RETURNTRANSFER => true,
  CURLOPT_ENCODING => "",
  CURLOPT_MAXREDIRS => 10,
  CURLOPT_TIMEOUT => 30,
  CURLOPT_HTTP_VERSION => CURL_HTTP_VERSION_1_1,
  CURLOPT_CUSTOMREQUEST => "GET",
  CURLOPT_HTTPHEADER => [
    "x-manus-api-key: <api-key>"
  ],
]);

$response = curl_exec($curl);
$err = curl_error($curl);

curl_close($curl);

if ($err) {
  echo "cURL Error #:" . $err;
} else {
  echo $response;
}

	req, _ := http.NewRequest("GET", url, nil)

	req.Header.Add("x-manus-api-key", "<api-key>")

x-codeSamples.label: Java
Ruby
x-codeSamples.source:
require 'uri'
require 'net/http'

url = URI("https://api.manus.ai/v2/task.listMessages?task_id=<string>&order=desc&limit=10")

http = Net::HTTP.new(url.host, url.port)
http.use_ssl = true

request = Net::HTTP::Get.new(url)
request["x-manus-api-key"] = '<api-key>'

response = http.request(request)
puts response.read_body

parameters.name: task_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the task to list messages for. Supports the shortcut `agent-default-main_task` for the IM agent's main task.

schema.description: Number of messages to return per page. Default: 50, Range: 1-200.
Pagination cursor from the previous response's next_cursor field. Omit for the first page.
Sort direction by timestamp. "desc" (default) returns newest first, "asc" returns oldest first (chronological order).

When true, includes detailed events: tool_used (tools the agent invoked), plan_update (full plan snapshots), new_plan_step (individual step additions), and explanation (agent reasoning). Default: false — only returns user_message, assistant_message, error_message, status_update, and user_stop.
Format for slides attachments in the response. "html" (default) returns raw HTML slides. "pptx" auto-converts HTML slides to PowerPoint format.

request_id.description: Unique identifier for this API request.

task_id.description: The task ID these messages belong to.

messages.description: Array of task event objects representing the conversation and agent activity.

has_more.description: Whether there are more messages beyond this page.

next_cursor.description: Cursor for fetching the next page. Only present when has_more is true.

post.description: Confirms a pending action to resume the task. Use when [task.listMessages](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/task.listMessages) shows `agent_status: "waiting"`. For `messageAskUser` events, use [task.sendMessage](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/task.sendMessage) instead. See the [Task Lifecycle](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/task-lifecycle#using-taskconfirmaction) guide for input formats.

task_id.description: The unique identifier of the task with a pending action. Supports the shortcut `agent-default-main_task` for the IM agent's main task.

event_id.description: The waiting_for_event_id value from the status_update event that triggered the confirmation request. This identifies which specific action to confirm.

input.description: Optional input data for the pending action. The expected schema is defined by the confirm_input_schema field in the status_update event. For simple confirmations, this can be omitted.

200.description: Action confirmed successfully. The task will resume processing.

request_id.description: Unique identifier for this API request.

task_id.description: The ID of the task that was confirmed.

confirmed.description: Always true when the confirmation was accepted.

post.description: Creates a new project. Projects let you group related tasks and apply shared instructions. Pass the returned ID as `project_id` in [task.create](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/task.create) to create tasks within this project.

instruction.description: Default instruction that will be automatically prepended to all tasks created within this project. Use this to enforce consistent behavior across related tasks (e.g., "Always respond in formal English" or "Focus on financial analysis").

request_id.description: Unique identifier for this API request.

get.description: Lists all projects. Use the returned IDs as `project_id` in [task.create](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/task.create) or [task.list](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/task.list).

request_id.description: Unique identifier for this API request.

get.description: Lists available skills. Includes project skills when project_id is provided. Use the returned IDs in `enable_skills` or `force_skills` of [task.create](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/task.create).

schema.description: When provided, includes project-specific skills in addition to the user's global skills. Use [project.list](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/project.list) to get available project IDs.

request_id.description: Unique identifier for this API request.

data.description: Array of skill objects. Use the id field when passing to enable_skills or force_skills in task.create.

get.description: Lists all custom agents in your account. Use the returned IDs in [task.list](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/task.list) with `scope=agent_subtask` to view subtasks, or in [agent.detail](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/agent.detail) for full details. See the [Agents](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/agents-overview) guide.

request_id.description: Unique identifier for this API request.

get.description: Retrieves an agent's details including its nickname, description, and associated task. Use [agent.update](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/agent.update) to modify, or [task.list](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/task.list) with `scope=agent_subtask` to view this agent's subtasks.

	url := "https://api.manus.ai/v2/agent.detail?agent_id=<string>"

	req, _ := http.NewRequest("GET", url, nil)

	req.Header.Add("x-manus-api-key", "<api-key>")

x-codeSamples.label: Java
Ruby
x-codeSamples.source:
require 'uri'
require 'net/http'

url = URI("https://api.manus.ai/v2/agent.detail?agent_id=<string>")

http = Net::HTTP.new(url.host, url.port)
http.use_ssl = true

request = Net::HTTP::Get.new(url)
request["x-manus-api-key"] = '<api-key>'

response = http.request(request)
puts response.read_body

parameters.name: agent_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the agent to retrieve.

request_id.description: Unique identifier for this API request.

agent.description: The agent object with full details.

post.description: Updates an agent's nickname and description. Use [agent.detail](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/agent.detail) to view current values before updating.

agent_id.description: The unique identifier of the agent to update.

nickname.description: New display name for the agent.

about.description: New description or bio for the agent.

request_id.description: Unique identifier for this API request.

post.description: Creates a file record and returns a presigned upload URL. Upload the file content to the returned `upload_url` via PUT before it expires. Then use the `file.id` as `file_id` in [task.create](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/task.create) or [task.sendMessage](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/task.sendMessage) message content.

filename.description: Name of the file to upload, including extension (e.g., "report.pdf", "data.csv"). The extension helps determine the file type.

request_id.description: Unique identifier for this API request.

file.description: The created file record. Status will be pending until the upload completes.

upload_url.description: Presigned S3 URL for uploading the file content. Send a PUT request with the file bytes as the body. Expires in 3 minutes.

upload_expires_at.description: Unix timestamp (seconds) when the upload_url expires. Complete the upload before this time.

get.description: Retrieves a file's details including upload status, size, and expiration time. Check that `status` is `uploaded` before using the file in [task.create](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/task.create). Files expire 48 hours after upload.

x-codeSamples.label: Java
Ruby
x-codeSamples.source:
require 'uri'
require 'net/http'

url = URI("https://api.manus.ai/v2/file.detail?file_id=<string>")

http = Net::HTTP.new(url.host, url.port)
http.use_ssl = true

request = Net::HTTP::Get.new(url)
request["x-manus-api-key"] = '<api-key>'

response = http.request(request)
puts response.read_body

parameters.name: file_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the file to retrieve.

request_id.description: Unique identifier for this API request.

file.description: The file object with full details.

post.description: Deletes a file. Files are automatically deleted 48 hours after upload, so manual deletion is only needed for early cleanup.

file_id.description: The unique identifier of the file to delete.

request_id.description: Unique identifier for this API request.

post.description: Creates a webhook for receiving task event notifications. Use [webhook.publicKey](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/webhook.publicKey) to get the key for verifying signatures. See the [Webhooks](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/webhooks-overview) guide for event types.

url.description: The HTTPS endpoint URL that will receive POST webhook notifications. Must be publicly accessible and return a 2xx status code.

request_id.description: Unique identifier for this API request.

get.description: Lists all webhooks in your account. Use the returned IDs with [webhook.delete](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/webhook.delete) to remove a webhook.

request_id.description: Unique identifier for this API request.

post.description: Deletes a webhook. The endpoint will stop receiving notifications immediately. Use [webhook.list](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/webhook.list) to find the webhook ID.

webhook_id.description: The unique identifier of the webhook to delete.

request_id.description: Unique identifier for this API request.

get.description: Lists the user's online browser clients. Use the returned `client_id` with [task.confirmAction](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/task.confirmAction) when the agent triggers a `needConnectMyBrowser` waiting event. See the [Task Lifecycle](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/task-lifecycle#using-my-browser) guide.

request_id.description: Unique identifier for this API request.

data.description: Array of online browser client objects.

get.description: Gets the public key for verifying webhook signatures. See the [Webhook Security](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/webhooks-security) guide for verification examples.

request_id.description: Unique identifier for this API request.

public_key.description: PEM-encoded RSA public key for verifying webhook signatures.

get.description: Lists the current user's credit consumption history. Each record corresponds to a task and shows the total credits consumed. Results are sorted by task creation time in descending order (newest first).

schema.description: Number of records to return per page. Default: 20, Max: 100.
Pagination cursor from the previous response's next_cursor field. Omit for the first page.
Filter start time as a Unix timestamp in seconds. Only returns records for tasks created at or after this time.
Filter end time as a Unix timestamp in seconds. Only returns records for tasks created at or before this time.

request_id.description: Unique identifier for this API request.

data.description: Array of credit consumption records, one per task.

has_more.description: Whether there are more records beyond this page. If true, use next_cursor to fetch the next page.

next_cursor.description: Cursor to pass as the cursor parameter for the next page. Only present when has_more is true.

get.description: Returns daily credit consumption totals for the team within a date range. Useful for building consumption trend charts. Admin/Owner sees the entire team's daily totals; Member sees only their own. Only available to team users — individual users receive a `permission_denied` error.

schema.description: Filter start time as a Unix timestamp in seconds.
Filter end time as a Unix timestamp in seconds.

request_id.description: Unique identifier for this API request.

data.description: Array of daily statistics sorted by date in ascending order.

get.description: Lists per-user daily credit consumption details for the team. Each record represents a specific user's consumption on a specific day. Results are sorted by time in descending order. Admin/Owner sees all members; Member sees only their own records. Only available to team users — individual users receive a `permission_denied` error.

schema.description: Number of records to return per page. Default: 20, Max: 100.
Pagination cursor from the previous response's next_cursor field. Omit for the first page.
Filter start time as a Unix timestamp in seconds.
Filter end time as a Unix timestamp in seconds.

request_id.description: Unique identifier for this API request.

data.description: Array of per-user daily consumption records.

has_more.description: Whether there are more records beyond this page. If true, use next_cursor to fetch the next page.

next_cursor.description: Cursor to pass as the cursor parameter for the next page. Only present when has_more is true.

get.description: Lists connectors installed in the current user's account. Use the returned IDs in the `connectors` array of [task.create](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/task.create) or [task.sendMessage](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/task.sendMessage). See the [Connectors](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/connectors) guide for setup.

request_id.description: Unique identifier for this API request.

data.description: Array of connectors installed in the user's account.

Message.description: A message to send to a task.

content.description: String (plain text) or array of ContentPart objects.

connectors.description: List of connector IDs to enable for this task. Resolution order when omitted: 1) if the task belongs to a project, the project's default connectors are used; 2) otherwise, the user's default enabled connectors are used. Use [connector.list](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/connector.list) to get the connectors installed in your account.

enable_skills.description: Skill IDs to enable for this task. If empty or omitted, loads the skills the user has enabled in their account settings. Use [skill.list](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/skill.list) to retrieve available skill IDs.

force_skills.description: Skill IDs the agent must invoke during this task. Forced skills are automatically available even if not listed in enable_skills.

text.description: The text content of the message.

oneOf.title: File
oneOf.description: **Provide the file via one of: file_id, file_url, or file_data.**

file_id.description: ID of a previously uploaded file. Upload via [file.upload](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/file.upload) first to get the file ID.

file_url.description: Publicly accessible URL to the file. The agent will download it directly.

filename.description: Display name of the file including extension (e.g., "report.pdf"). Required when using file_data; recommended for file_url.

mime_type.description: MIME type of the file (e.g., "application/pdf", "image/png"). Optional; auto-detected from filename if omitted.

oneOf.title: Voice
oneOf.description: **Provide the audio via one of: file_id, file_url, or file_data.**

file_id.description: ID of a previously uploaded audio file. Upload via [file.upload](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/file.upload) first to get the file ID.

file_url.description: Publicly accessible URL to the audio file. The agent will download and transcribe it.

filename.description: Display name of the audio file including extension (e.g., "recording.wav"). Required when using file_data.

mime_type.description: MIME type of the audio file (e.g., "audio/wav", "audio/mp3"). Optional; auto-detected from filename if omitted.

Task.description: Represents a task with its current status and metadata.

status.description: Current task status. "running" — agent is actively working. "stopped" — task has finished or been stopped. "waiting" — agent is paused and waiting for user input or confirmation. "error" — task encountered an unrecoverable error.

created_at.description: Unix timestamp (seconds) when the task was created.

updated_at.description: Unix timestamp (seconds) when the task was last updated.

task_type.description: Type of the task. "standard" — a regular standalone task. "project" — a task within a project. "agent_subtask" — a subtask created by an agent. Use [task.list](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/task.list) with `scope` to filter by task type.

share_visibility.description: Who can view the task. "private" — only the task creator. "team" — all team members. "public" — anyone with the share link.

credit_usage.description: Total credits consumed by the task. Only present when the task has consumed credits.

task_url.description: URL to view the task in the Manus webapp (e.g., https://manus.im/app/{task_id}).

created_by_api_key.description: The API key that created this task. Present when the task was created via the API; null or absent when created through the UI or other means. The name reflects the API key's current name, not a snapshot from creation time.

name.description: The current display name of the API key.

TaskEvent.description: A task event representing a message, status change, or agent activity. Only one payload field (user_message, assistant_message, etc.) will be present, determined by the type field.

type.description: Event type. Determines which payload field is present. tool_used, plan_update, new_plan_step, and explanation are only included when verbose=true.

timestamp.description: Unix timestamp (seconds) when this event occurred.

user_message.description: Present when type="user_message". Contains a message sent by the user.

content.description: The text content of the user's message.

message_type.description: How the message was sent. "text" for typed input, "voice" for voice input.

attachments.description: Files or images the user attached to their message.

assistant_message.description: Present when type="assistant_message". Contains a response from the agent.

content.description: The text content of the agent's response.

attachments.description: Files, images, or slides the agent generated as output.

error_message.description: Present when type="error_message". Indicates the task encountered an error.

status_update.description: Present when type="status_update". Indicates a change in the agent's processing state.

agent_status.description: The agent's current state. "waiting" means the agent needs user confirmation — check status_detail for the event to confirm.

status_detail.description: Additional details when agent_status is "waiting". Contains the event ID needed for task.confirmAction.

waiting_for_event_id.description: The event ID to pass as event_id when calling task.confirmAction.

waiting_description.description: Human-readable description of what the agent is waiting for.

confirm_input_schema.description: JSON Schema defining the expected input format for the confirmation, if additional input is needed.

brief.description: Short summary of the current agent activity.

description.description: Detailed description of what the agent is doing.

tool_used.description: Present when type="tool_used" (verbose=true only). Records a tool invocation by the agent.

tool.description: Name of the tool that was used (e.g., "browser", "code_executor").

action_id.description: Unique identifier for this tool action.

brief.description: Short summary of the tool action.

description.description: Detailed description of what the tool did.

message.description: Structured details of the tool invocation.

action.description: The specific action performed by the tool.

param.description: Parameters passed to the tool action.

plan_update.description: Present when type="plan_update" (verbose=true only). A full snapshot of the agent's current execution plan.

steps.description: All steps in the current plan with their statuses.

status.description: Current status of this plan step.

started_at.description: Unix timestamp when this step started. Null if not yet started.

end_at.description: Unix timestamp when this step finished. Null if not yet completed.

new_plan_step.description: Present when type="new_plan_step" (verbose=true only). A new step was added to the plan.

step_id.description: Unique identifier for the new plan step.

title.description: Description of the new plan step.

explanation.description: Present when type="explanation" (verbose=true only). The agent's internal reasoning or thought process.

content.description: The agent's explanation of its reasoning or approach.

TaskAttachment.description: A file or media attachment in a task message.

type.description: Type of attachment. "image" for images, "file" for documents, "voice" for audio recordings, "slides" for presentation slides.

filename.description: Display name of the attachment file.

url.description: URL to download the attachment content.

Project.description: A project for organizing tasks with shared instructions.

created_at.description: Unix timestamp (seconds) when the project was created.

instruction.description: Default instruction applied to all tasks in this project.

Skill.description: A skill that extends the agent's capabilities.

id.description: Unique identifier for the skill. Use this in enable_skills or force_skills when creating tasks.

description.description: What the skill does and when it's useful.

owner_type.description: Who owns the skill. "personal" — user-created skill. "official" — built-in system skill. "team" — team-shared skill. "project" — project-specific skill.

creator_info.description: Information about who created the skill.

user_id.description: User ID of the skill creator.

name.description: Display name of the skill creator.

created_at.description: Unix timestamp (seconds) when the skill was created.

updated_at.description: Unix timestamp (seconds) when the skill was last updated.

Agent.description: A custom agent with its own identity (nickname and description) that can be customized for specific use cases. See the [Agents](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/agents-overview) guide.

id.description: Unique identifier for the agent. Use this as `agent_id` in [task.list](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/task.list) (with `scope=agent_subtask`) to view this agent's subtasks.

task_id.description: The task ID associated with this agent's creation or configuration. Use [task.detail](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/task.detail) to view the associated task.

nickname.description: Display name of the agent. Can be updated via [agent.update](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/agent.update).

about.description: Description or bio of the agent explaining its purpose and specialization. Can be updated via [agent.update](https://open.manus.ai/docs/v2/agent.update).

File.description: Basic file information returned in lists and upload responses.

id.description: Unique identifier for the file. Use this as file_id when attaching to tasks.

status.description: File status. "pending" — waiting for upload. "uploaded" — ready to use. "deleted" — file has been deleted. "error" — upload failed.

created_at.description: Unix timestamp (seconds) when the file record was created.

FileDetail.description: Full file information returned by file.detail.

status.description: File status. "pending" — waiting for upload. "uploaded" — ready to use. "deleted" — file has been deleted. "error" — upload failed.

created_at.description: Unix timestamp (seconds) when the file record was created.

bytes.description: File size in bytes. Only available after upload is complete.

expires_at.description: Unix timestamp (seconds) when the file will be automatically deleted (48 hours after upload).

error_message.description: Error description if the file status is "error".

Webhook.description: A webhook subscription for receiving task event notifications.

url.description: The endpoint URL receiving webhook POST notifications.

created_at.description: Unix timestamp (seconds) when the webhook was created.

BrowserClient.description: An online browser client connected to the user's account.

client_id.description: Unique identifier for the browser client.

client_name.description: Display name of the browser client.

id.description: Unique identifier for the connector. Use this as the connector ID when creating tasks.

type.description: Connector type. "builtin" — built-in connector provided by Manus. "byok" — bring-your-own-key connector. "mcp" — MCP (Model Context Protocol) connector.

description.description: Human-readable description of the connector's functionality.

UsageRecord.description: Credit consumption record for a single task.

credits.description: Total credits consumed by this task.

created_at.description: Task creation time as a Unix timestamp in seconds.

date.description: Date as a Unix timestamp in seconds, representing 00:00:00 of that day.

credits.description: Total credits consumed on this day.

date.description: Date as a Unix timestamp in seconds, representing 00:00:00 of that day.

credits.description: Credits consumed by this user on this day.

ErrorResponse.description: Standard error response format returned when a request fails.

request_id.description: Unique identifier for this API request, useful for debugging with support.

message.description: Human-readable error description explaining what went wrong.
